A ring buffer offers superior performance and predictability for telemetry ingestion due to its fixed-size, contiguous memory layout, which eliminates dynamic allocation overhead and cache misses. Under bounded memory constraints, it provides constant-time O(1) enqueue and dequeue operations with minimal CPU jitter, making it ideal for high-throughput, latency-sensitive scenarios where data loss is acceptable or managed by overwriting oldest entries. Its simplicity also reduces the risk of memory fragmentation and pointer-chasing penalties inherent in dynamic structures.
